must_not: call Number() or parseFloat() on input_decimal
coerce input string to a JS number for any intermediate step
must: reject inputs not matching /^-?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)(\.[0-9]+)?$/ with NON_PLAIN_DECIMAL_INPUT
tokenize matched inputs into (sign, integer_digits, fractional_digits) as digit-string arrays
check magnitude against plain_decimal_range; emit PLAIN_DECIMAL_OUT_OF_SCOPE if outside
locate the significant-digit window from the first non-zero digit
inspect the guard digit (10th sig digit) for the rounding decision
apply round-half-to-even on the digit array, propagating carries digit-by-digit
emit a plain-decimal string with trailing zeros padded to 9 sig figs
normalize negative zero to positive zero before emission

out_of_scope_behavior: Inputs whose value falls outside plain_decimal_range produce expected_error PLAIN_DECIMAL_OUT_OF_SCOPE. Inputs not in plain-decimal format produce expected_error NON_PLAIN_DECIMAL_INPUT. These categories are not interchangeable.
range_rationale: v0.3: min_magnitude = 1e-12 is sized to admit numeric_policy.tolerance hybrid-form atol default (= 1e-12) without falling back to scientific notation. v0.1/v0.2 used 1e-9 (sized for the pure-atol Mazur tolerance); widened in v0.3 to admit tighter atol values. Data values (gradients, weights, signals, losses, inputs) sit well above this floor in practice; the floor exists to keep configuration constants emittable.

cases.note: Tie at 10th sig digit; 9th = 9 (odd), round up, carries to 0
Tie at 10th sig digit; 9th = 1 (odd), round up to 2 (even)
Symmetric: round magnitude under HTE, reapply sign
Tie, round up; 9 -> 10 carries through adjacent 9, lifts 7th digit
Carries all the way through to next order of magnitude
Carries through ones place; digit count shifts but sig-fig count holds at 9
Leading zero before first sig digit doesn't count; 9 sig figs from the 5
Tie at 10th sig digit; 9th = 9 (odd), round up, carries
v0.1/v0.2 minimum (1e-9); still in scope under v0.3 [1e-12, 1e7)
Valid plain decimal; magnitude (1e-10) — was OUT_OF_SCOPE in v0.1/v0.2, now in scope under v0.3 [1e-12, 1e7)
v0.3 minimum supported plain-decimal magnitude (1e-12); required for numeric_policy.tolerance hybrid-form atol default

Valid plain decimal; magnitude (1e-14) below v0.3 plain_decimal_range.min_magnitude (1e-12). The 1e-13 strings format cleanly because IEEE-754 representation of 1e-12 dips to leading-exponent -13; the format module's pre-round check accepts -13 to admit 1e-12 round-trips, so genuine OUT_OF_SCOPE rejections start at -14 or beyond.
Scientific notation; policy formatter rejects non-plain-decimal inputs before evaluating value
